id: "13.3.7"
title: "Distributed Trace Sampling Strategy Coverage and Bias Detection (head versus tail rates, OTel Splunk Distribution and tailsamplingprocessor, Tempo, AWS X-Ray rules, Datadog APM dynamic sampling, traceparent loss, clock skew)"

# UC-13.3.7 · Distributed Trace Sampling Strategy Coverage and Bias Detection (head versus tail rates, OTel Splunk Distribution and tailsamplingprocessor, Tempo, AWS X-Ray rules, Datadog APM dynamic sampling, traceparent loss, clock skew)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We run every customer request through a checkpoint like inspectors at a factory door. When the gate opens too rarely or skips the cracked boxes, we think we are safe while broken items slip past; when the rules ignore errors and slow paths, the worst shipments never get inspected.*

---

## Description

Detects mis-tuned head versus tail sampling rates, sampling-driven coverage gaps per service and operation, error and critical-path under-sampling, sampler policy regression after rollouts, dynamic sampling drift, traceparent loss across Kafka SNS and SQS edges, and probabilistic sampler clock skew by correlating APM trace metrics, OTLP span events, collector tail-sampling internal metrics, Observability Cloud sampling counters, and edge access log denominators enriched with declared sampling policy and CMDB ownership.

## Value

Platform and product leaders receive defensible evidence when observability looks healthy yet forensic coverage quietly thins: cost campaigns, precedence mistakes, reservoir misconfiguration, or header loss at messaging edges can hide errors and latency tails that executives still assume are visible. Reliability governance gains a single correlation narrative that separates probability loss from structural propagation loss and routes teams to the correct vendor console or YAML change without days of debate.

## Implementation

Land APM trace metrics, otel:trace spans, collector tail-sampling internal metrics, Observability Cloud sampling counters, and edge access logs into Splunk; maintain apm_sampling_policy.csv and cmdb service_inventory; save observability_uc_13_3_7_sampling_coverage_bias on a five-minute cadence with earliest=-2h@h; route critical and high severities to platform observability on-call; archive weekly CSV exports.

## Evidence

Saved search observability_uc_13_3_7_sampling_coverage_bias on a five-minute schedule; exported CSV joined to apm_sampling_policy.csv; dashboard drilldowns across apm_metrics, otel:trace, otel_metrics, signalfx_apm_sampling, and access logs archived to a restricted observability evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab tenant, lower effective head sampling for a canary service while preserving log traffic, confirm observability_uc_13_3_7_sampling_coverage_bias returns critical or high within two five-minute windows with sampling_yield_pct below half of lookup expected_sampling_rate and non-null recommended_action.

### Negative scenario

After restoring declared sampling rates and validating traceparent across Kafka and SNS in lab, when tail late-span counters normalize and log denominators align, confirm severities return to low or medium baselines and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability architects, APM operators, and service owners who set sampling budgets across Splunk APM, Splunk Observability Cloud, the Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector, upstream opentelemetry-collector-contrib gateways, Grafana Tempo, AWS X-Ray, and Datadog APM. This use case is deliberately about sampling policy effectiveness: whether declared head and tail sampling rates, error oversampling promises, and vendor-specific reservoirs actually produce representative trace coverage for each service, operation, and region—not whether individual spans link structurally to parents, which remains the focus of UC-13.5.3, and not OpenTelemetry Collector pipeline queue saturation in isolation, which remains UC-13.3.15.

Sampling is a statistical contract between cost and fidelity. Head sampling decides early, often via parent-based compositors wrapping trace ID ratio samplers, and can silently discard rare error paths before tail policies ever see them. Tail sampling waits for trace completion (subject to decision and span wait timeouts), evaluates ordered policies such as status_code, latency, string_attribute, probabilistic, and rate_limiting, and only then exports a coherent subset. The Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector documents how to configure these processors for production; misordered policy lists, overly aggressive global probabilistic gates, or rate_limiting policies that trip during incidents can drown the very traces you need for diagnosis.

Grafana Tempo and similar trace backends amplify cardinality cliffs: when sampling jumps from one effective rate to another after a rollout, ingestion hotspots and block compactions can lag while dashboards still imply steady coverage. AWS X-Ray expresses sampling through rules, service reservoirs, and centralized reservoir borrowing; under-budgeted reservoirs in burst regions look like healthy sampling in the console while Splunk proves yield collapse against log-derived denominators. Datadog APM applies dynamic sampling and ingestion controls that adapt to volume and retention; finance-driven caps can lower effective sample rates for low-QPS services disproportionately unless error and latency policies compensate.

Trace context propagation underpins honest yield math. W3C Trace Context defines traceparent and tracestate; probabilistic samplers depend on trace ID randomness and clock discipline. Kafka, Amazon SNS, and Amazon SQS bridges drop linkage when middleware strips headers, rewrites keys, or uses binary payloads that omit injected fields—yield falls not because probabilities changed but because downstream spans form unrelated fragments that no longer match log denominators keyed on route and service.

Tail sampling processors maintain in-memory decision caches keyed by trace ID; collector pod replacement, horizontal shard changes, or hash collisions under extreme fan-out erase cache warmth and can recreate late-span drops when decision_wait is shorter than cross-region RTT. Clock skew between ingress log timestamps, broker headers, and OTLP export times biases latency-threshold policies and can mark healthy spans as late, triggering discard paths that resemble policy regression.

RBAC and privacy posture: sampling policy lookups and exemplar traces may reference customer cohorts, pricing tiers, or internal service tiers. Restrict apm_sampling_policy.csv to least-privilege roles, hash high-cardinality route names when counsel requires minimization, and never paste raw HEC or Observability Cloud tokens into saved searches. Legal review should confirm whether error oversampling on sensitive operations remains compliant when it increases retention of payloads.

Differentiation recap: UC-13.3.7 measures sampling yield, drift, error-path coverage, tail late-span rates, and vendor rule precedence against declared policy—not Prometheus scrape health (UC-13.3.2), not alert fanout storms (UC-13.3.5), not collector exporter queue saturation without sampling context (UC-13.3.15), and not structural orphan spans or missing roots (UC-13.5.3). When yield collapses, pair this control with UC-13.5.3 to separate propagation loss from probability loss.

Risk briefing: short flaps after collector rollouts routinely shift yield for minutes; streamstats windows enforce sustained deviation before critical severity. Regional traffic shifts change log denominators; require business_priority and environment tiers in routing so tier-two services do not page during expected seasonality. RUM session sampling is orthogonal; do not conflate browser session quotas with server trace yield without explicit joins.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands Splunk APM or vendor-normalized trace request metrics in index=apm_metrics (or equivalent metrics index) with metric_name trace.requests and dimensional tags service.name, span.name, and deployment.environment so mstats arms can compute request-scale denominators comparable to trace counts.

Lane B lands OTLP-flattened span events in index=apm_traces, index=spans, or index=otel with sourcetype=otel:trace, preserving trace_id, span attributes for service.name, name or http.route, deployment.environment, otel.status_code, is_recording or sampling.priority analogs, and any sampling.rule_name your pipeline adds after tail decisions.

Lane C scrapes OpenTelemetry Collector internal metrics into index=otel_metrics, focusing on otelcol_processor_tail_sampling families that expose sampled traces, dropped-too-early decisions, and late span counters; label with service_instance_id to distinguish gateway pools.

Lane D mirrors Splunk Observability Cloud ingestion-side sampling counters into index=signalfx_apm_sampling (name per your TA) with fields such as traces_seen, sampled_traces, tail_decisions, and late_span_drops when exported via HEC or cloud export jobs.

Lane E provides log-derived request denominators from index=web, index=istio, or index=ingress using sourcetype=access_combined, istio_access, or aws:alb:accesslogs; normalize service_name and path keys to join operation labels used in APM.

Maintain apm_sampling_policy.csv keyed on service and operation with expected_sampling_rate, error_oversample_rate, owner_team, runbook_url, and business_priority. Refresh weekly from GitOps or service catalog exports. Maintain cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory rows for supplemental region and tier labels joined on service.

props.conf should lowercase trace_id hex, normalize duration fields, and map deployment.environment consistently. For X-Ray mirrored events, map rule_name and reservoir fields into sampling.rule_name when dual-publishing to Splunk. For Datadog mirrored spans, retain dd.trace.sampled and ingestion_reason when your bridge provides them.

Example readiness checks: export a canary trace across Kafka with explicit traceparent headers and confirm yield_pct stays within five points of expected_sampling_rate for tier-zero services. Induce a harmless tail decision_wait reduction in non-production and confirm decision_late_span_rate rises in Splunk before production rollout.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_3_7_sampling_coverage_bias with a five-minute schedule during steady production, dispatch.earliest_time=-2h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after volume guards on apm_req_sum. Throttle duplicate service, operation, and env tuples for twenty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, sampling_yield_pct, and decision_late_span_rate in pager narratives so incident commanders open tail-sampling YAML, X-Ray rule consoles, or Datadog ingestion controls without guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans APM mstats, raw otel:trace spans, collector tail-sampling internal metrics, Observability Cloud sampling counters, and edge access log denominators so a silent lane cannot hide policy regression. coalesce() ladders absorb missing service, operation, env, and sampler_name fields across bridges. stats aggregates sums and perc99 latency weights per bucket. streamstats applies twelve five-minute buckets for one-hour context and six buckets for thirty-minute min yield. eventstats supplies per-environment medians for baseline hints. inputlookup apm_sampling_policy.csv joins declared targets; join to cmdb service_inventory enriches ownership. case() encodes criticality when sustained yield falls below half the target percentage with volume, when error_sample_rate lags the error_oversample_rate contract, or when decision_late_span_rate exceeds five percent.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.3.7 sampling strategy coverage bias APM OTel tail X-Ray Datadog multisearch earliest=-2h@h bucket=5m streamstats_1h=12")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=apm_metrics metric_name="trace.requests" span=5m BY service.name span.name deployment.environment
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',"apm_metric_unknown"))))
      | eval operation=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('span.name',"all_operations"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('deployment.environment',"unknown_env"))))
      | eval observed_request_count_apm=round(coalesce(mv,0),4)
      | eval observed_trace_count_apm=0
      | eval observed_request_count_log=0
      | eval error_span_total=0
      | eval error_span_sampled=0
      | eval tail_late_span_events=0
      | eval tail_decision_total=0
      | eval p99_latency_trace_weight=0
      | eval lane="apm_trace_requests_mstats"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats max(observed_request_count_apm) AS observed_request_count_apm BY _time service operation env lane ]
    [ search (index=apm_traces OR index=spans OR index=otel) sourcetype=otel:trace earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,"otel_trace_unknown"))))
      | eval operation=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('name',span_name,http_route,operation,"all_operations"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_env"))))
      | eval sampler_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('sampling.rule_name',sampling_rule_name,sampler_name,"otel_tail_or_head"))))
      | eval is_err=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce('otel.status_code',otel_status_code,status.code,""))),"error") OR tonumber(coalesce('status.code',status_code,0),10)>=2,1,0)
      | eval is_rec=if(tonumber(coalesce(is_recording,sampled,1),10)==1,1,0)
      | eval dur_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round('duration.nanos'/1000000,4),0),10)
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats dc(trace_id) AS observed_trace_count_apm count AS span_rows sum(eval(is_err)) AS error_span_total sum(eval(if(is_err==1 AND is_rec==1,1,0))) AS error_span_sampled perc99(dur_ms) AS p99_latency_trace_weight BY _time service operation env
      | eval observed_request_count_apm=0
      | eval observed_request_count_log=0
      | eval tail_late_span_events=0
      | eval tail_decision_total=0
      | eval lane="otel_trace_raw_spans"
      | eval sampler_name="otel_trace_event_lane"
      | fields _time service operation env sampler_name observed_request_count_apm observed_trace_count_apm observed_request_count_log error_span_total error_span_sampled tail_late_span_events tail_decision_total p99_latency_trace_weight lane ]
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=otel_metrics (metric_name="otelcol_processor_tail_sampling_sampling_trace_dropped_too_early" OR metric_name="otelcol_processor_tail_sampling_sampling_trace_sampled" OR metric_name="otelcol_processor_tail_sampling_sampling_span_count_late_spans") span=5m BY service_instance_id metric_name
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_instance_id,"otel_tail_collector"))))
      | eval operation="tail_sampling_aggregate"
      | eval env="collector_plane"
      | eval tail_late_span_events=if(match(metric_name,"late"),mv,0)
      | eval tail_decision_total=if(match(metric_name,"sampled|too_early"),mv,0)
      | eval observed_request_count_apm=0
      | eval observed_trace_count_apm=0
      | eval observed_request_count_log=0
      | eval error_span_total=0
      | eval error_span_sampled=0
      | eval p99_latency_trace_weight=0
      | eval lane="otelcol_tail_sampling_internal"
      | eval sampler_name="tailsamplingprocessor"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(tail_late_span_events) AS tail_late_span_events sum(tail_decision_total) AS tail_decision_total BY _time service operation env lane sampler_name ]
    [ search index=signalfx_apm_sampling earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sf_service,service,"sooc_sampling_unknown"))))
      | eval operation=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(operation_name,span_name,"all_operations"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(environment,env,"unknown_env"))))
      | eval observed_trace_count_apm=tonumber(coalesce(traces_ingested,sampled_traces,0),10)
      | eval observed_request_count_apm=tonumber(coalesce(traces_seen,root_requests,0),10)
      | eval tail_late_span_events=tonumber(coalesce(late_span_drops,0),10)
      | eval tail_decision_total=tonumber(coalesce(tail_decisions,0),10)
      | eval error_span_total=0
      | eval error_span_sampled=0
      | eval observed_request_count_log=0
      | eval p99_latency_trace_weight=0
      | eval lane="signalfx_apm_sampling_counters"
      | eval sampler_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sampler_policy,sfx_sampler,"observability_cloud"))))
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(observed_trace_count_apm) AS observed_trace_count_apm sum(observed_request_count_apm) AS observed_request_count_apm sum(tail_late_span_events) AS tail_late_span_events sum(tail_decision_total) AS tail_decision_total BY _time service operation env lane sampler_name ]
    [ search (index=web OR index=istio OR index=ingress) (sourcetype=access_combined OR sourcetype=istio_access OR sourcetype=aws:alb:accesslogs) earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service,"gateway_log_unknown"))))
      | eval operation=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(http_uri,uri,path,"all_operations"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(environment,env,"unknown_env"))))
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats count AS observed_request_count_log BY _time service operation env
      | eval observed_request_count_apm=0
      | eval observed_trace_count_apm=0
      | eval error_span_total=0
      | eval error_span_sampled=0
      | eval tail_late_span_events=0
      | eval tail_decision_total=0
      | eval p99_latency_trace_weight=0
      | eval lane="edge_access_log_denominator"
      | eval sampler_name="log_ground_truth"
      | fields _time service operation env sampler_name observed_request_count_apm observed_trace_count_apm observed_request_count_log error_span_total error_span_sampled tail_late_span_events tail_decision_total p99_latency_trace_weight lane ]
| eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,"unknown_service"))))
| eval operation=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(operation,"all_operations"))))
| eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,"unknown_env"))))
| eval sampler_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sampler_name,"unspecified_sampler"))))
| stats sum(observed_request_count_apm) AS apm_req_sum sum(observed_trace_count_apm) AS trace_sum sum(observed_request_count_log) AS log_req_sum sum(error_span_total) AS err_tot sum(error_span_sampled) AS err_samp sum(tail_late_span_events) AS late_sum sum(tail_decision_total) AS dec_sum max(p99_latency_trace_weight) AS p99_latency_sample_count values(lane) AS lane_mv values(sampler_name) AS sampler_mv BY _time service operation env
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lane_mv),0)
| eval sampler_name=mvindex(mvdedup(sampler_mv),0)
| eval denom=coalesce(log_req_sum, apm_req_sum, 0)
| eval sampling_yield_pct=if(denom>0, round(100*trace_sum/denom,3), null())
| eval error_traffic_pct=if(err_tot>0, 1, 0)
| eval error_sample_rate=if(err_tot>0, round(err_samp/err_tot,4), null())
| eval decision_late_span_rate=if(dec_sum>0, round(100*late_sum/dec_sum,3), 0)
| sort 0 service operation env _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f first(sampling_yield_pct) AS yield_lag_1h BY service operation env
| streamstats window=6 current=t global=f min(sampling_yield_pct) AS min_yield_30m BY service operation env
| eventstats median(sampling_yield_pct) AS env_median_yield BY env
| eventstats median(apm_req_sum) AS env_median_req BY env
| join type=left max=0 service operation
    [| inputlookup apm_sampling_policy.csv
     | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_name,""))))
     | eval operation=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(operation,span_operation,"all_operations"))))
     | fields service operation expected_sampling_rate error_oversample_rate owner_team runbook_url business_priority ]
| join type=left max=0 service
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_id,""))))
      | dedup service
      | fields service cmdb_region service_tier ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned_service")
| eval business_priority=coalesce(business_priority,"tier_unrated")
| eval expected_sampling_rate=tonumber(coalesce(expected_sampling_rate,0.1),10)
| eval error_oversample_rate=tonumber(coalesce(error_oversample_rate,1.0),10)
| eval target_yield_pct=round(100*expected_sampling_rate,3)
| eval sampling_drift_pct=if(isnotnull(sampling_yield_pct), round(sampling_yield_pct-target_yield_pct,3), null())
| eval err_expect=min(1.0, coalesce(error_oversample_rate,1.0)*0.95)
| eval severity=case(
    min_yield_30m>0 AND min_yield_30m<(0.5*target_yield_pct) AND apm_req_sum>=50, "critical",
    err_tot>=20 AND coalesce(error_sample_rate,0)<(0.95*err_expect), "critical",
    decision_late_span_rate>5, "critical",
    coalesce(sampling_yield_pct,0)<(0.7*target_yield_pct) AND apm_req_sum>=30, "high",
    decision_late_span_rate>2, "high",
    abs(coalesce(sampling_drift_pct,0))>15, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND min_yield_30m<(0.5*target_yield_pct), "rollback_head_or_tail_sampling_rates_validate_traceidratio_and_parentbased_policies_per_service",
    severity=="critical" AND err_tot>=20, "restore_error_oversample_rules_validate_status_code_and_string_attribute_tail_policies",
    severity=="critical" AND decision_late_span_rate>5, "increase_tail_sampling_decision_wait_rebalance_collector_shards_validate_kafka_sns_sqs_traceparent_preservation",
    severity=="high", "audit_sampling_rule_precedence_xray_reservoir_datadog_dynamic_caps_and_otel_filter_dropping_sampling_priority",
    severity=="medium", "tune_baseline_thresholds_review_cost_campaign_metadata_and_regional_traffic_shifts",
    true(), "observe_continue_calibration_compare_tempo_retention_and_rum_session_correlation")
| table _time, service, operation, env, owner_team, business_priority, expected_sampling_rate, sampling_yield_pct, error_sample_rate, decision_late_span_rate, p99_latency_sample_count, severity, recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_3_7_sampling_coverage_bias_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -2h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-observability-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Sampling drift $result.severity$ $result.service$ $result.operation$ yield=$result.sampling_yield_pct$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_3_7_sampling_coverage_bias | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if multisearch queue time exceeds internal objectives, materialize each lane into five-minute summary indexes keyed on service, operation, and env, widen alerts to earliest=-90m on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For high-cardinality routes, aggregate operations to template patterns in lookup keys before indexing.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Splunk APM or Observability Cloud trace explorers: pick a tier-zero service, compare trace counts in-window to log denominators for the same route, and confirm sampling_yield_pct matches manual division within one collection interval. When Splunk disagrees with the vendor UI, verify indexer latency, duplicate indexing of mirrored bridges, and field aliasing on deployment.environment.

Validate mstats lane against the vendor metrics UI for trace.requests or equivalent series. Validate otel:trace lane by comparing dc(trace_id) to the explorer count for the same filter. Validate tail-sampling metrics by curling collector self-telemetry on a gateway pod and comparing counters to otel_metrics rows.

Validate propagation hypotheses by capturing Kafka record headers in a non-production consumer and confirming traceparent survives producers through SNS or SQS fan-out. Validate X-Ray by comparing rule priority lists and reservoir usage in the AWS console to late_span counters. Validate Datadog by reviewing ingestion sampling controls and legacy agent head sampling settings still pinned on hosts.

Synthetic drill: lower head sampling tenfold in lab, confirm critical severity on yield within two windows, then restore prior rates and confirm decay. Negative test: on calm traffic with stable policies, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when volume floors are tuned.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Deploy lowered head sampling tenfold and error paths vanish: restore parent-based compositor defaults, add tail status_code policies, and validate trace ID ratio seeds before closing the incident.

Case 2 — Tail-sampling decision_wait too short causes late-span drops: increase decision_wait under load tests, shard traces by tenant, and verify cross-AZ RTT fits inside wait budgets.

Case 3 — rate_limiting policy hits during an outage and drowns representative samples: raise limits temporarily with incident commander approval, separate incident burst pools from steady state, and document new ceilings in apm_sampling_policy.csv.

Case 4 — Per-service rule overridden by a global probabilistic gate: reorder tail policies so error and latency predicates precede global sampling, and remove duplicate head sampling that negates tail decisions.

Case 5 — Collector pod replaced and decision cache lost: use session affinity where supported, warm caches with canaries after rollouts, and stagger replacements across gateway pools.

Case 6 — Kafka transport drops traceparent headers and fragments samples: fix serializer configuration, enforce header allow lists on brokers, and replay canary messages through SNS and SQS paths.

Case 7 — AWS X-Ray reservoir under-budgeted in burst regions: increase reservoir targets, borrow centralized reservoir capacity, and align rules with regional QPS forecasts.

Case 8 — Datadog APM dynamic sampling lowered for cost capping: negotiate tiered retention, restore error retention policies, and map finance campaigns to lookup business_priority for muted routing.

Case 9 — Service onboarding uses legacy SDK head sampling only: migrate to OpenTelemetry parent-based compositors, add gateway tail sampling, and block go-live until yield_pct meets tier policy.

Case 10 — A/B-test cohort under-sampled relative to control: encode cohort labels in string_attribute policies, exclude experiment keys from global probabilistic denominators, and validate parity in yield dashboards.

Case 11 — Cross-region traceparent corruption during VPN restart: pause rollouts, validate edge proxies, and extend decision_wait until RTT stabilizes.

Case 12 — Faulty filter drops sampling.priority attributes and collapses yield: remove aggressive attribute drops, validate transform processors, and replay shadow traffic to confirm restoration.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, multisearch, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists thirteen operational columns including severity and recommended_action for routing.


Teaching notes for junior engineers: always separate probability from propagation. Probability loss shows up as yield divergence against logs while structural completeness (UC-13.5.3) may still look acceptable when head sampling simply never admits error spans. Conversely, perfect sampling probabilities cannot fix missing traceparent headers at SNS boundaries.

Finance partners: tie sampling_yield_pct trends to invoice lines for Observability Cloud and Datadog by exporting weekly CSVs from this saved search filtered to business_priority tier_one. Executives should read drift as forensic risk, not vanity metrics.

Runbook cross-links belong in apm_sampling_policy.csv runbook_url fields: Splunk tail sampling processor docs, AWS X-Ray sampling console guidance, Datadog ingestion mechanism pages, Grafana Tempo operational limits, and internal Kafka header standards.

For hybrid fleets, dual-publish a subset of spans to Tempo for TraceQL checks while Splunk remains system of record for alerting; confirm dual export does not double-count denominators when comparing mstats lanes.

Post-incident reviews should ask whether rate_limiting policies were tripped, whether clock skew voided latency sampling, and whether global probabilistic fractions moved during a cost campaign without updating apm_sampling_policy.csv.

Capacity planners should model tail-sampling memory against worst-case trace fan-out per gateway, not average QPS, because shard collisions and cache loss spike simultaneously during incidents.

Security teams should review who can edit sampling YAML in GitOps; unauthorized merges can look like attacks on observability even when motives are benign cost tuning.

SRE teams should pair this UC with RUM correlation only after aligning session sampling to trace sampling narratives; otherwise executives misinterpret dips as customer outages.

Vendor support bundles should include redacted YAML, five-minute multisearch outputs, and exemplar trace IDs with proof of yield math.

For serverless workloads, cold starts inflate late spans; maintain separate env labels and relax decision_late_span_rate thresholds with architecture sign-off.

For mainframe bridges, timestamps may arrive in batches; widen buckets to ten minutes when testing those lanes to avoid false criticals.

For multi-cloud VPN meshes, document which regions share collectors; imbalanced shards can bias yield per region even when global rates look correct.

Audit evidence: archive weekly CSV exports of critical and high rows to a restricted observability_evidence index with change ticket references when thresholds move.

Continuous improvement: when OpenTelemetry releases new tail-sampling policies, revalidate metric names in otel_metrics and update FIELDALIAS maps in the TA.

Stakeholder communication: product owners care about error visibility; translate severity rows into probable blind spots using error_sample_rate and err_tot.

Tooling: optional Splunk ITSI modules can map services to entities when KPIs consume the same metrics indexes, but this UC remains valuable without premium modules.

Long-term architecture: evaluate separate gateway pools for high-cardinality edge services versus stable core APIs to keep tail caches bounded.

IPv6-only environments must validate traceparent carriage through dual-stack load balancers; yield gaps often trace to header normalization bugs rather than probabilities.

Service meshes may inject baggage that explodes attribute cardinality; trim before tail evaluation to keep policy CPU predictable.

Finally, human judgment still validates automated severity; this search guides investigations, not replaces context during customer-impacting outages.




Teaching notes for junior engineers: always separate probability from propagation. Probability loss shows up as yield divergence against logs while structural completeness (UC-13.5.3) may still look acceptable when head sampling simply never admits error spans. Conversely, perfect sampling probabilities cannot fix missing traceparent headers at SNS boundaries.

Finance partners: tie sampling_yield_pct trends to invoice lines for Observability Cloud and Datadog by exporting weekly CSVs from this saved search filtered to business_priority tier_one. Executives should read drift as forensic risk, not vanity metrics.

Runbook cross-links belong in apm_sampling_policy.csv runbook_url fields: Splunk tail sampling processor docs, AWS X-Ray sampling console guidance, Datadog ingestion mechanism pages, Grafana Tempo operational limits, and internal Kafka header standards.

For hybrid fleets, dual-publish a subset of spans to Tempo for TraceQL checks while Splunk remains system of record for alerting; confirm dual export does not double-count denominators when comparing mstats lanes.

Post-incident reviews should ask whether rate_limiting policies were tripped, whether clock skew voided latency sampling, and whether global probabilistic fractions moved during a cost campaign without updating apm_sampling_policy.csv.

Capacity planners should model tail-sampling memory against worst-case trace fan-out per gateway, not average QPS, because shard collisions and cache loss spike simultaneously during incidents.

Security teams should review who can edit sampling YAML in GitOps; unauthorized merges can look like attacks on observability even when motives are benign cost tuning.

SRE teams should pair this UC with RUM correlation only after aligning session sampling to trace sampling narratives; otherwise executives misinterpret dips as customer outages.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.3.7 sampling strategy coverage bias APM OTel tail X-Ray Datadog multisearch earliest=-2h@h bucket=5m streamstats_1h=12")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=apm_metrics metric_name="trace.requests" span=5m BY service.name span.name deployment.environment
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',"apm_metric_unknown"))))
      | eval operation=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('span.name',"all_operations"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('deployment.environment',"unknown_env"))))
      | eval observed_request_count_apm=round(coalesce(mv,0),4)
      | eval observed_trace_count_apm=0
      | eval observed_request_count_log=0
      | eval error_span_total=0
      | eval error_span_sampled=0
      | eval tail_late_span_events=0
      | eval tail_decision_total=0
      | eval p99_latency_trace_weight=0
      | eval lane="apm_trace_requests_mstats"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats max(observed_request_count_apm) AS observed_request_count_apm BY _time service operation env lane ]
    [ search (index=apm_traces OR index=spans OR index=otel) sourcetype=otel:trace earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,"otel_trace_unknown"))))
      | eval operation=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('name',span_name,http_route,operation,"all_operations"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"unknown_env"))))
      | eval sampler_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('sampling.rule_name',sampling_rule_name,sampler_name,"otel_tail_or_head"))))
      | eval is_err=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce('otel.status_code',otel_status_code,status.code,""))),"error") OR tonumber(coalesce('status.code',status_code,0),10)>=2,1,0)
      | eval is_rec=if(tonumber(coalesce(is_recording,sampled,1),10)==1,1,0)
      | eval dur_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round('duration.nanos'/1000000,4),0),10)
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats dc(trace_id) AS observed_trace_count_apm count AS span_rows sum(eval(is_err)) AS error_span_total sum(eval(if(is_err==1 AND is_rec==1,1,0))) AS error_span_sampled perc99(dur_ms) AS p99_latency_trace_weight BY _time service operation env
      | eval observed_request_count_apm=0
      | eval observed_request_count_log=0
      | eval tail_late_span_events=0
      | eval tail_decision_total=0
      | eval lane="otel_trace_raw_spans"
      | eval sampler_name="otel_trace_event_lane"
      | fields _time service operation env sampler_name observed_request_count_apm observed_trace_count_apm observed_request_count_log error_span_total error_span_sampled tail_late_span_events tail_decision_total p99_latency_trace_weight lane ]
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=otel_metrics (metric_name="otelcol_processor_tail_sampling_sampling_trace_dropped_too_early" OR metric_name="otelcol_processor_tail_sampling_sampling_trace_sampled" OR metric_name="otelcol_processor_tail_sampling_sampling_span_count_late_spans") span=5m BY service_instance_id metric_name
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_instance_id,"otel_tail_collector"))))
      | eval operation="tail_sampling_aggregate"
      | eval env="collector_plane"
      | eval tail_late_span_events=if(match(metric_name,"late"),mv,0)
      | eval tail_decision_total=if(match(metric_name,"sampled|too_early"),mv,0)
      | eval observed_request_count_apm=0
      | eval observed_trace_count_apm=0
      | eval observed_request_count_log=0
      | eval error_span_total=0
      | eval error_span_sampled=0
      | eval p99_latency_trace_weight=0
      | eval lane="otelcol_tail_sampling_internal"
      | eval sampler_name="tailsamplingprocessor"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(tail_late_span_events) AS tail_late_span_events sum(tail_decision_total) AS tail_decision_total BY _time service operation env lane sampler_name ]
    [ search index=signalfx_apm_sampling earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sf_service,service,"sooc_sampling_unknown"))))
      | eval operation=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(operation_name,span_name,"all_operations"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(environment,env,"unknown_env"))))
      | eval observed_trace_count_apm=tonumber(coalesce(traces_ingested,sampled_traces,0),10)
      | eval observed_request_count_apm=tonumber(coalesce(traces_seen,root_requests,0),10)
      | eval tail_late_span_events=tonumber(coalesce(late_span_drops,0),10)
      | eval tail_decision_total=tonumber(coalesce(tail_decisions,0),10)
      | eval error_span_total=0
      | eval error_span_sampled=0
      | eval observed_request_count_log=0
      | eval p99_latency_trace_weight=0
      | eval lane="signalfx_apm_sampling_counters"
      | eval sampler_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sampler_policy,sfx_sampler,"observability_cloud"))))
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats sum(observed_trace_count_apm) AS observed_trace_count_apm sum(observed_request_count_apm) AS observed_request_count_apm sum(tail_late_span_events) AS tail_late_span_events sum(tail_decision_total) AS tail_decision_total BY _time service operation env lane sampler_name ]
    [ search (index=web OR index=istio OR index=ingress) (sourcetype=access_combined OR sourcetype=istio_access OR sourcetype=aws:alb:accesslogs) earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service,"gateway_log_unknown"))))
      | eval operation=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(http_uri,uri,path,"all_operations"))))
      | eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(environment,env,"unknown_env"))))
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats count AS observed_request_count_log BY _time service operation env
      | eval observed_request_count_apm=0
      | eval observed_trace_count_apm=0
      | eval error_span_total=0
      | eval error_span_sampled=0
      | eval tail_late_span_events=0
      | eval tail_decision_total=0
      | eval p99_latency_trace_weight=0
      | eval lane="edge_access_log_denominator"
      | eval sampler_name="log_ground_truth"
      | fields _time service operation env sampler_name observed_request_count_apm observed_trace_count_apm observed_request_count_log error_span_total error_span_sampled tail_late_span_events tail_decision_total p99_latency_trace_weight lane ]
| eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,"unknown_service"))))
| eval operation=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(operation,"all_operations"))))
| eval env=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,"unknown_env"))))
| eval sampler_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sampler_name,"unspecified_sampler"))))
| stats sum(observed_request_count_apm) AS apm_req_sum sum(observed_trace_count_apm) AS trace_sum sum(observed_request_count_log) AS log_req_sum sum(error_span_total) AS err_tot sum(error_span_sampled) AS err_samp sum(tail_late_span_events) AS late_sum sum(tail_decision_total) AS dec_sum max(p99_latency_trace_weight) AS p99_latency_sample_count values(lane) AS lane_mv values(sampler_name) AS sampler_mv BY _time service operation env
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lane_mv),0)
| eval sampler_name=mvindex(mvdedup(sampler_mv),0)
| eval denom=coalesce(log_req_sum, apm_req_sum, 0)
| eval sampling_yield_pct=if(denom>0, round(100*trace_sum/denom,3), null())
| eval error_traffic_pct=if(err_tot>0, 1, 0)
| eval error_sample_rate=if(err_tot>0, round(err_samp/err_tot,4), null())
| eval decision_late_span_rate=if(dec_sum>0, round(100*late_sum/dec_sum,3), 0)
| sort 0 service operation env _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f first(sampling_yield_pct) AS yield_lag_1h BY service operation env
| streamstats window=6 current=t global=f min(sampling_yield_pct) AS min_yield_30m BY service operation env
| eventstats median(sampling_yield_pct) AS env_median_yield BY env
| eventstats median(apm_req_sum) AS env_median_req BY env
| join type=left max=0 service operation
    [| inputlookup apm_sampling_policy.csv
     | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_name,""))))
     | eval operation=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(operation,span_operation,"all_operations"))))
     | fields service operation expected_sampling_rate error_oversample_rate owner_team runbook_url business_priority ]
| join type=left max=0 service
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_id,""))))
      | dedup service
      | fields service cmdb_region service_tier ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned_service")
| eval business_priority=coalesce(business_priority,"tier_unrated")
| eval expected_sampling_rate=tonumber(coalesce(expected_sampling_rate,0.1),10)
| eval error_oversample_rate=tonumber(coalesce(error_oversample_rate,1.0),10)
| eval target_yield_pct=round(100*expected_sampling_rate,3)
| eval sampling_drift_pct=if(isnotnull(sampling_yield_pct), round(sampling_yield_pct-target_yield_pct,3), null())
| eval err_expect=min(1.0, coalesce(error_oversample_rate,1.0)*0.95)
| eval severity=case(
    min_yield_30m>0 AND min_yield_30m<(0.5*target_yield_pct) AND apm_req_sum>=50, "critical",
    err_tot>=20 AND coalesce(error_sample_rate,0)<(0.95*err_expect), "critical",
    decision_late_span_rate>5, "critical",
    coalesce(sampling_yield_pct,0)<(0.7*target_yield_pct) AND apm_req_sum>=30, "high",
    decision_late_span_rate>2, "high",
    abs(coalesce(sampling_drift_pct,0))>15, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND min_yield_30m<(0.5*target_yield_pct), "rollback_head_or_tail_sampling_rates_validate_traceidratio_and_parentbased_policies_per_service",
    severity=="critical" AND err_tot>=20, "restore_error_oversample_rules_validate_status_code_and_string_attribute_tail_policies",
    severity=="critical" AND decision_late_span_rate>5, "increase_tail_sampling_decision_wait_rebalance_collector_shards_validate_kafka_sns_sqs_traceparent_preservation",
    severity=="high", "audit_sampling_rule_precedence_xray_reservoir_datadog_dynamic_caps_and_otel_filter_dropping_sampling_priority",
    severity=="medium", "tune_baseline_thresholds_review_cost_campaign_metadata_and_regional_traffic_shifts",
    true(), "observe_continue_calibration_compare_tempo_retention_and_rum_session_correlation")
| table _time, service, operation, env, owner_team, business_priority, expected_sampling_rate, sampling_yield_pct, error_sample_rate, decision_late_span_rate, p99_latency_sample_count, severity, recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance host=* earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS apm_gateway_host
| join type=left max=0 apm_gateway_host [| inputlookup apm_sampling_policy.csv | eval apm_gateway_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,"")))) | fields apm_gateway_host owner_team business_priority]
| where like(lower(apm_gateway_host),"%otel%") OR like(lower(apm_gateway_host),"%apm%") OR like(lower(apm_gateway_host),"%gateway%")
| table apm_gateway_host cpu_load mem_used owner_team business_priority
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by service, operation, and env with drilldowns to otel:trace rows, otel_metrics tail-sampling series, signalfx_apm_sampling counters, access logs, and joined policy columns; sparklines for sampling_yield_pct and decision_late_span_rate.

## Known False Positives

Planned cost-capping campaigns that lower global sample rates are legitimate when documented in change calendars; downgrade severity using business_priority and finance ticket metadata before paging service owners. Weekend low-traffic services produce noisy denominators; require apm_req_sum floors so quiet services do not trigger false criticals. Regional traffic shifts after marketing launches inflate log counts without proportional trace volume; compare against env_median_yield before declaring incidents. A/B test cohorts intentionally under-sampled relative to control per experiment design; join experiment labels and exclude cohort keys from default alerting. Infra-side benchmark routing that bypasses application gateways yields log denominators without matching APM service keys; filter benchmark paths in lookup tables. Blue-green cutovers duplicate traces briefly across mirrored indexes; deduplicate trace_id in investigations before muting. Chaos drills that strip Kafka headers will trip yield detectors; annotate drill namespaces. Vendor maintenance windows that pause ingestion mimic drift; check status pages before rollback. Stale apm_sampling_policy.csv rows after service renames misstate targets; refresh lookups weekly. Dual HEC mirrors that double-count access logs inflate denominators; normalize source types per environment.

- [OpenTelemetry — Sampling](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/sampling/)
- [OpenTelemetry Collector Contrib — Tail sampling processor](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector-contrib/tree/main/processor/tailsamplingprocessor)
- [Splunk Docs — Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector](https://docs.splunk.com/observability/en/gdi/opentelemetry/opentelemetry.html)
- [AWS Docs — Sampling rules for AWS X-Ray](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/xray/latest/devguide/xray-console-sampling.html)
- [Datadog Docs — Trace ingestion mechanisms](https://docs.datadoghq.com/tracing/trace_pipeline/ingestion_mechanisms/)
- [W3C — Trace Context](https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/)
- [Google Research — Adaptive sampling for distributed traces](https://research.google/pubs/pub49997/)
- [Grafana Docs — Tempo ingestion limits](https://grafana.com/docs/tempo/latest/operations/ingestion_limit/)
